spec_match.rationale: Uses getWeekInfo() / getCalendars() (the new methods) with a fallback to the legacy getter when the method is missing — exactly the right migration pattern, and demonstrates the real payoff (locale-aware calendar rendering).

interactive.rationale: Locale dropdown drives a live month grid that re-rotates columns and shades weekend cells based on the live week-info values.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'render a real month grid that rotates its columns and shades its weekend based on getWeekInfo(). Try Arabic, Hebrew, Persian locales.' Page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Falls back to `{ firstDay: 1, weekend: [6, 7], minimalDays: 1 }` when both method and legacy getter are missing — silent. A 'using fallback for locale X' note would help debugging. Also: the page only renders the gregory calendar; the calendars[] surface includes islamic, hebrew, persian etc. which are the actual reason for the locale-info API. Not rendering a non-gregorian month is a missed pedagogical opportunity.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5148228059398144 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only; weekend cells use --accent-rose or similar accent.

openQuestions.title: Render a non-Gregorian calendar when the locale prefers one
openQuestions.detail: For ar-SA-u-ca-islamic-umalqura, fa-IR-u-ca-persian, he-IL-u-ca-hebrew the calendar should swap to that calendar system (using Intl.DateTimeFormat with calendar option). Right now the page rotates columns but keeps Gregorian month numbers — which is the half of the API the page is built to teach.

openQuestions.title: Show the underlying API call in a side panel
openQuestions.detail: A code block updated per-render showing `locale.getWeekInfo()` → `{ firstDay: 7, weekend: [5, 6], minimalDays: 1 }` would make the data-flow explicit and let visitors copy the snippet for their own apps.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'fallback used' indicator
openQuestions.detail: When neither getWeekInfo nor weekInfo is present the page silently uses defaults. Surface a small warning so users on browsers without the API know what they're seeing.

openQuestions.title: Cover the getTextInfo() direction property
openQuestions.detail: The grid should also flip RTL for Arabic/Hebrew/Persian locales — getTextInfo().direction would drive that. Currently the page rotates the week start but ignores text direction.

summary: Solid use-case demo for getWeekInfo() column rotation. Two upgrades that would make this the strongest concept in the feature: render the locale's preferred calendar (islamic/persian/hebrew) not just Gregorian, and flip RTL on locales where getTextInfo().direction is 'rtl'.
